notes: All five consistency checks pass on logical/textual grounds. The original_text is verbatim from the real J&J FY2025 10-K, confirmed via EDGAR full-text search and direct document fetch. The wrong_directionality hallucination is correctly constructed: royalties rose from $2.0B (FY2024) to $2.4B (FY2025), but modified_text uses 'eased to... from' to imply the $2.4B figure fell from $2.0B, and the claim compounds this with 'softened... down from'. Subtlety rating 4 is appropriate since both dollar figures are preserved verbatim and only the directional framing is inverted. However, the source metadata is fabricated: real filing is CIK 0000200406 (not 19617), accession 0000200406-26-000016 (not 000162828026008131), document jnj-20251228.htm (not jpm-20251231.htm). The source_url points to a JPMorgan-ticker-named file that does not exist for J&J. Despite the wrong metadata, the original_text content itself is authentic and verbatim from the real filing."
suggested_correction: Correct source_filing to 'Johnson & Johnson 10-K (FY2025), filed under CIK 0000200406, accession 0000200406-26-000016'. Correct source_url to 'https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/200406/000020040626000016/jnj-20251228.htm'. The CIK 19617 and accession 000162828026008131 in the original case are fabricated; the jpm-20251231.htm filename is a JPMorgan artifact and does not exist in J&J's filing. All other fields (original_text, modified_text, claim, hallucination_type, verdict, subtlety) are correct and validated.
haiku_reason: The claim uses "softened to... down from" language suggesting a decrease, but the numbers show an increase from $2.0B to $2.4B (+20%). The directional language directly contradicts the numerical data, making this a self-evident misrepresentation regardless of source verification.
